title: SACRAMENTO, CA –

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California produces 4% of the world’s olive oil from over 75 varieties of olives.",
    "There has been an increase in branding of olive oil with the term 'California' when the product is blended using oils sourced from other regions.",
    "A bottle of olive oil may be branded as 'California' but actually come from other countries.",
    "This misbranding has led to consumer confusion and places California olive farmers at a competitive disadvantage.",
    "AB 2074 makes it illegal to falsely represent that an olive oil is produced entirely from olives grown in California.",
    "The bill prohibits representations indicating that an olive oil was produced from olives grown in a specific region of California unless at least 85% of the oil is produced from that region.",
    "The bill aims to ensure that when 'California' is used on a product, it is accurate and reflects true sourcing.",
    "AB 2074 provides consumers with clear information about what they are purchasing."
  ]
}
```
